item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In light of this discussion on O5 Command, I'd like to bring attention to my use of "amnesiac" here in a way I feel makes sense in-universe, and would like to explicitly request that any changes not be made to this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting and very realistically applied antimeme. Any crime syndicate would kill for something like this. I love the way you used outdated terms, too, definitely adding that to my headcanon. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meyeehh seee.
Lord I adore this time period, and the concept of a safe-house being an anti-meme is pretty good.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always like a good antimeme and enjoy the story around this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Overall good atmosphere, but if there's any one reason I upvoted, it's for finding a legitimate way to use "amnesiacs". :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The antimeme itself isn't super novel, but I enjoyed it's application in mob dynamics and the historical setting enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely adore the use of [sic] in this article, and while it lacks a bit of the character found in other Chicago Spirit articles, it's still a fun historical romp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In keeping with my "ObserverSeptember tries writing for GoIs" trend, here's my take on the Chicago Spirit! What better way to get around Prohibition than create a speakeasy police can't remember?
Alternative title: "Someone Snitched".

The words marked "[sic] - RAISA" are intentionally inaccurate - like us, I headcanon the Foundation as using older terms before eventually switching over to modern ones.

Image sources: 1, 2. Image #1 is labeled for reuse, image #2 is public domain in the USA (pre-1923).
